question_title: Will ASML’s Q1 2026 results show gross margin strictly above 52.0%?
summary: ASML guided Q1 2026 gross margin at 51%-53%, placing the 52.0% threshold exactly at the midpoint of their guidance range. This makes the question close to a coin flip as a starting point. Recent 2025 quarterly margins averaged ~52.6% (with all four quarters at or above 51.6%), and analyst consensus for FY2026 leaned toward the upper half of guidance (~52.9%). ASML also slightly beat Q4 2025 margin estimates. These factors provide a mild positive tilt. However, Q1 2026 has lower guided revenue (€8.2-8.9B vs €9.7B in Q4), restructuring charges from 1,700 layoffs could affect margins, and ASML's guidance itself implies some expected margin compression vs 2025 (52.8% actual). Monte Carlo simulation centered at ~52.1% with appropriate uncertainty yields ~56% probability of exceeding 52.0%.

factors.description: ASML Q1 2026 guidance midpoint is exactly 52.0% (range 51-53%), placing the threshold at the exact midpoint of guidance - essentially a coin flip starting point
ASML's recent quarterly margins (Q1'25: 54.0%, Q2'25: 53.7%, Q3'25: 51.6%, Q4'25: 52.2%) show margins tend to cluster above 52%
Analyst consensus for FY2026 GM was ~52.9%, suggesting analysts expect the upper half of the guidance range
ASML beat Q4 2025 margin estimate (52.2% vs 51.9% estimate), suggesting mild positive guidance bias
Q1 2026 revenue guidance (€8.2-8.9B) is lower than Q4 2025 (€9.7B), suggesting fewer system deliveries which could affect product mix and margin
1,700 layoff restructuring announced in Jan 2026 could create one-time charges affecting reported gross margins
Trade/tariff uncertainty and geopolitical tensions could impact margins through supply chain costs

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.56 (56% probability YES)**

The key insight is that ASML's own Q1 2026 gross margin guidance of 51%-53% places the 52.0% threshold exactly at the midpoint. This starts as a ~50% base rate. The mild upward tilt to 56% reflects:

- **Recent margins lean above 52%**: All four 2025 quarters averaged ~52.9%, suggesting ASML's operations naturally deliver in this range
- **Analyst expectations lean high**: Street consensus at ~52.9% for FY2026 implies they expect the upper half of guidance
- **Mild positive guidance bias**: ASML beat Q4 margin estimates

Partially offset by lower Q1 revenue guidance, potential restructuring charges, and acknowledged margin pressure in the 2026 outlook. ASML reports Q1 2026 on April 14, 2026.
sources_consulted: [ASML reports €32.7 billion total net sales and €9.6 billion net income in 2025](https://www.asml.com/en/news/press-releases/2026/q4-2025-financial-results)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if ASML’s first official Q1 2026 financial results release reports gross margin strictly above 52.0%, as shown on ASML Investor Relations at https://www.asml.com/investors/financial-results
fine_print: Use ASML’s first official Q1 2026 results release and the gross margin figure reported for the quarter itself. Ignore later revisions unless ASML clearly states that the original figure was erroneous. Candidate Sources: ASML Financial Calendar https://www.asml.com/investors/financial-calendar
